contract.name: v2/autocomplete/city.md
contract.description: Returns autocomplete suggestions for city names filtered by recipient or place-of-performance scope.

search_text.description: Free-text prefix used to match city names (case-insensitive).
search_text.constraints: Required string with at least one character; empty string rejected with 422; whitespace-only values are accepted and return broad result sets; null rejected with 400.

limit.description: Maximum number of suggestions to return.
limit.constraints: Optional (defaults to 10 when omitted); accepts integers 0-500 inclusive; numeric strings with optional surrounding whitespace are coerced to integers; floats, null, or non-numeric strings return 400; negative integers shrink the dataset and counts, and magnitudes near or below -100 trigger 503 responses with empty bodies.

filter.description: Location constraints for the autocomplete; extra keys are ignored.

country_code.constraints: Required; case-insensitive; null rejected with 400; unknown codes return zero matches without error.

scope.description: Indicates whether to match recipient locations or primary places of performance.
scope.constraints: Required; must be exactly 'recipient_location' or 'primary_place_of_performance'; any other casing or value returns 400.

state_code.description: Optional domestic state or foreign region filter.
state_code.constraints: Optional; null treated as omitted; empty string rejected with 422; unrecognized strings simply yield zero matches.

filter.constraints: Required object; omitting, null, or empty filter returns 422.

count.description: Total number of matching cities; negative limits can reduce this reported total due to backend offset handling.

results.description: Array of city matches capped by the requested limit.

city_name.description: Matched city name (uppercase in observed responses).

state_code.description: State, territory, or foreign region string associated with the city; observed as uppercase text with no nulls.

body.detail: Invalid value in 'limit'. 'None' is not a valid type (integer)

quirks: Omitting limit defaults to 10 results even though documentation labels it required.
Numeric strings for limit are coerced to integers after trimming whitespace, while floats or non-numeric strings return 400 errors.
Negative limit values shrink the returned rows and reported count; values near -100 or smaller produce 503 responses with empty bodies.
Whitespace-only search_text values are accepted and yield broad, high-volume result sets.
state_code values have only been observed as uppercase strings (including ISO-like tokens for foreign matches); no nulls have appeared in sampled results.
Additional keys in filter are ignored by the backend.
filter.scope comparisons are case-sensitive and reject values outside the two documented options.
risks: Clamp limit to the documented 0-500 range and avoid negative values to prevent truncated data or 503 errors.
Large limits (e.g., 500) produce sizable payloads without pagination metadata, which may impact client performance.
Whitespace or very short search_text inputs can return huge generalized datasets; clients may need to trim or validate user input.
503 errors return empty bodies, so callers must handle the absence of diagnostic detail.
gaps: Documentation allows results[].state_code to be null, but no probes observed null values; unclear if null ever occurs in practice.
Backend rationale and exact threshold for 503 responses on negative limits remain unknown beyond the observed failure near -100.
No pagination mechanism is exposed; counts above 500 imply additional matches that cannot be retrieved via this endpoint alone.

probes.notes: Valid USA recipient search scoped to VA returns a single Springfield entry.
Omitting limit defaults to 10 returned rows while count reports all 47 foreign matches.
Whitespace-only search text is accepted and returns broad nationwide results.
Limit 500 is accepted and returns the maximum observed page while count remains higher than the returned rows.
Large negative limit shrinks the reported count and yields an empty result set.
Negative limit near -120 triggers a 503 with no response body.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'limit'. 'None' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: Null limit is rejected with a 400 type error.
Scope comparisons are case-sensitive; mixed-case value returns 400.
Empty filter object produces 422 requiring country_code and scope.

mismatches: Docs say filter is optional, but the API requires filter.country_code and filter.scope (empty filter returns 422).
Docs require limit, yet the API accepts missing limit and defaults to 10.
Docs describe limit as a number, but the API enforces an integer, rejecting floats while coercing numeric strings.
Docs omit any maximum for limit, yet the API enforces an upper bound of 500.
gaps: Documentation allows results[].state_code to be null, but no probes observed null values; unclear if null ever occurs in practice.
Backend rationale and exact threshold for 503 responses on negative limits remain unknown beyond the observed failure near -100.
No pagination mechanism is exposed; counts above 500 imply additional matches that cannot be retrieved via this endpoint alone.
risks: Clamp limit to the documented 0-500 range and avoid negative values to prevent truncated data or 503 errors.
Large limits (e.g., 500) produce sizable payloads without pagination metadata, which may impact client performance.
Whitespace or very short search_text inputs can return huge generalized datasets; clients may need to trim or validate user input.
503 errors return empty bodies, so callers must handle the absence of diagnostic detail.
